description: Existing eval set with mixed provenance, used to test incremental merge behavior.

evals.name: feat-routing-new-capability
evals.prompt: I added a --json output flag to the CLI. Draft a commit message.
evals.expected_output: A Conventional Commits message using the feat type, imperative subject under 50 chars.
expectations: Response chooses the feat type because the change adds a new user-facing capability.
[auto] Response subject line is in imperative mood.

evals.name: breaking-change-footer
evals.prompt: [auto] The format() function now returns an object instead of a string. Write the commit.
evals.expected_output: A commit that marks the breaking change with a ! on the type and a BREAKING CHANGE footer.
expectations: [auto] Response marks the type with an exclamation mark.
[auto] Response includes a BREAKING CHANGE footer.

evals.name: manual-multi-scope-monorepo
evals.prompt: I touched packages/api and packages/web in one staged change set. How should I scope the commit?
evals.expected_output: Human-authored eval: the skill should recommend splitting into separate commits or using a broad scope, and must not invent a single misleading scope.
expectations: Response recommends either splitting the change into separate commits or using a neutral scope rather than fabricating one scope that hides the second package.
Response explains the reasoning in terms of commit atomicity.
